channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: No grass cutting as been done this year., 29th May
item.description:
Lamppost 22 on the corner of Rookery Rd/Innsworth Lane opposite Technology Park the grass as not been cut this year at all. It will be that tall soon that I will not be able to get to my fence or windows etc.. Why is it not being maintained when in 20 yrs it was previously done by TBC ? Smaller areas have been done in the area.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rookery Road, Innsworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bus Stop has been battered, 29th May
item.description:
Hi Matt The Cheltenham Spa Railway Station Gloucester Road stop has graffiti on 3 windows and then 1 smashed window. Clearly happened since updating the new timetable

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gloucester Road, Warden Hill, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees desperately need pruning, emergency services unable to view CCTV of the street clearing onscuring view of CCTV in that area., 29th May
item.description:
After making a police report today I learned that the trees along the promenade are obscuring a view of their CCTV in this area because of the overgrown trees. They therefore may not spot or be able to follow perpetrators of crime on their CCTV causing a distinct safety and security inside black spot in this area. Urgent action needed to prune back the trees in both the area adjacent to the old cavendish house building outside Oliver bones and fat face and also along the promenade by the bus and taxi rank.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Promenade, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Visibility poor - dangerous on 'give way' both sides, 29th May
item.description:
This has been reported previously and highway deemed not an issue - we live just behind this and are constantly hearing vehicles sounding their horns and screeching of breaks. You cannot clearly see whether there is oncoming traffic from both sides of the highway whilst travelling due to the grass verges being so overgrown. This must be resolved asap to avoid a serious RTC.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gotherington Lane, Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Replacement of bus stop, 29th May
item.description:
Thank you for removing the dangerous damaged bus stop outside the Exmouth Arms on the Bath Road GL53 7LX Stagecoach have referred me to you to find out when the replacement will be installed. Thank you in advance & I look forward to hearing from you Helen
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 165, Bath Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree & vegetation, 29th May
item.description:
This tree and general overgrown surroundings need cutting all along the 435 on this stretch walking is interrupted by the overspill of vegetation. Specifically the tree in the photo requires limbo skills to be used. This reduces visibility and is dangerous. WHY cannot the vegetation be cut before it has grown, as this would be a quicker operation, and cost less. and not be so unsightly. I just reported this on fix my street and tewkesbury offered your group as the responsible authority.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A435, Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 12" pothole on pavement, 29th May
item.description:
1 sterling court, at corner of road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7-9, Sterling Court, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 8 ft long pothole - was marked with a large white dot last year by Highways. Due to rain and mud which remain in the hole this marker eventually disappeared. The other pothole by No19 was marked today with a box - it had a round dot last year the same as ours. No 10 had a box drawn today around a very small pothole which is the same depth as the one outside No 8 which is also very much longer., 29th May
item.description:
On road outside Number 8 New Barn Close. The pothole has existed for a number of years and been reported before. Last year Highways marked it with a large white dot alongside another pothole a bit further up in the middle of the road. Today that pothole was marked for repair but not the longer one outside No 8. Can you advise of the reason, please? A much smaller pothole outside No 10 was also marked for repair. It has the same depth as the one outside No 8. So not sure why the much larger hole was not selected. Because the rainwater cannot drain off in the large pothole, mud also collects and fills the hole. The pavement is regularly flooded and the water meter on the pavement is inaccessible and under water then. This pothole outside No 8 stops rainwater from reaching the drain just behind and causes regular issues when there is strong rainfall. Could you please advise why this pothole was selected last year but has not been marked for repair today? As mentioned above, the white dot mark disappeared under the water and mud over a few months. Many thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, New Barn Close, Lynworth, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 29th May
item.description:
15db Pothole CW 500x450x60mm approx Road closure with 15db Stats (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Inear drainage channel/gully that’s been asphalted over rather than properly reinstated., 29th May
item.description:
The drainage channel outside my property, 29 Albert Place, appears to have been improperly filled with tarmac rather than repaired or reinstated. As this channel carries roof and surface water runoff, I am concerned that the obstruction may breach the Highway Authority’s obligations under the Highways Act 1980 to maintain adequate highway drainage and may create a risk of flooding, damp penetration, icing, and damage to both the pavement and adjoining property. I request that this be treated as a formal highways drainage defect and inspected urgently.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Albert Place, Marle Hill, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Grass not cut for 2 months., 29th May
item.description:
The grass on the green and surrounding areas has not been cut for two months (apart from a resident who tried with his small mower) We've lived here 27 years and the grass has always been cut regularly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cadbury Close, Hucclecote, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road sign left behind. Reported this 3 months ago, case closed 2 days ago without collecting, 29th May
item.description:
Before the old farmers arms pub (about 50- 100 metres) see photo

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A435, Gotherington, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Signs & Barriers left behind by Contractor

item.title: Incorrect Signage, 29th May
item.description:
I’d like to report some cycling signage that has been put up incorrectly by the alley between Selkirk Gardens and Whaddon Road. I’ve attached two photos the illustrate this. The signs are currently showing the opposite direction to the true one. The two signs just need to be turned 180 degrees and they’ll be fine.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Grosvenor Place South, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
